Notes:
Originally from Greenville, SC, I started my first business at the age of fifteen offering web development services as I learned and grew with the web in the mid-1990s and later went on to study Law at the University of Michigan. During my tenure there, I continued to grow my business and technology knowledge (and pay for college) through offering IT services.

After college, I spent over a decade offering a full range of technology services & advice to some of the biggest organizations throughout the world. 

I formed coderPros in 2005 and currently reside in Europe where I continue to run and expand the business and travel fairly frequently to meet with clients around the globe, because business - even in the digital age - is still about building and nurturing relationships, and that cannot yet be done entirely over the internet.

Oh dear. It seems my other post was deleted due to its inflammatory nature :(

Perhaps I shall try to make nice, yes?

I've never quite found that magical woman I've been searching for. Oh,
I have a picture in my mind, but this imagining never quite comes to
fruition. I truly don't believe that there is a 6' plus young woman
out there who could talk my ear off about punk, politics, her cat, and
the first time he played Chrono Trigger, or Final Fantasy, or the
Illusion of Gaia.

So, facebook, I have resorted to you to help me find someone I can
actually stand!

In short, I love people who are intelligent. This does not include
people who merely say they are intelligent. I make no pretenses about
who or what I am, and expect no less from any person I'm at all
interested in.

That said, you should be able to/should:

- hold a conversation about things that weren't fed to you by your
television (this includes music, books, and oh, opinions and such)
- have a sense of humour (the more abrasive and inappropriate, | Oh dear. It seems my other post was deleted due to its inflammatory nature :(

Perhaps I shall try to make nice, yes?

I've never quite found that magical woman I've been searching for. Oh,
I have a picture in my mind, but this imagining never quite comes to
fruition. I truly don't believe that there is a 6' plus young woman
out there who could talk my ear off about punk, politics, her cat, and
the first time he played Chrono Trigger, or Final Fantasy, or the
Illusion of Gaia.

So, facebook, I have resorted to you to help me find someone I can
actually stand!

In short, I love people who are intelligent. This does not include
people who merely say they are intelligent. I make no pretenses about
who or what I am, and expect no less from any person I'm at all
interested in.

That said, you should be able to/should:

- hold a conversation about things that weren't fed to you by your
television (this includes music, books, and oh, opinions and such)
- have a sense of humour (the more abrasive and inappropriate, the better)
- have ambition and be passionate about SOMETHING

Bonus points will be awarded for:

- geeking out over SNES RPGS
- geeking out over the Replacements and the Nils and the birth of grunge
- reading books/watching films that have nothing to do with Michael Moore
- helping me out with the Saturday/Sunday crosswords when I get fed up
with them

Multipliers will be applied for:

- cuddling while watching bad Marvel Comics movie adaptations
- hardcore makeouts
- baking cupcakes
- breaking out musical instruments and playing horrible crust songs
while screaming at the tops of our lungs
- being taller than I am

As for me, I possess all of the qualities and meet the requirements
listed above (save for the height, har har). Perhaps you have a list
of your own to share?

Physcially, I'm rather tall (5'11"), and am quite far from skinny; a
fact the round shape I call my torso will attest to. My love for baked
goods far outweighs my desire to have tiny hips and no ass. I suppose
I would be referred to as geeky, meaning that people probably believe
they can tell what music I listen to and what my political stance is
just by looking at me. I have that look with the bangs and the glasses
and the clothes that everyone who has ever purchased an album with the
subheading "POST GRUNGE SCREAMOXCORE" from HMV seems to love. Oh well.
At least my hair isn't dyed black (anymore)? Haaaaaha.

If anything listed above sounds at all like you, or you wish to know
more, feel free to reply. Give me a shout even if you read through
this opus, if you like. A picture for a picture sounds like a fair
trade, yes?

Later, gents.
